report_name: LinkedIn Accused of Secretly Scanning for 6,000+ Browser Extensions

attack_patterns.name: Browser Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: LinkedIn's website allegedly loads a JavaScript file that checks for the presence of over 6,200 installed browser extensions by attempting to load resources using the `chrome-extension://` URL scheme, effectively fingerprinting the user's browser and collecting information about installed extensions.

campaigns.name: BrowserGate
campaigns.description: A data collection practice reported by Fairlinked e.V. where LinkedIn allegedly scans user browsers for thousands of extensions. The stated purpose by LinkedIn is security against data scraping, while the report alleges the motive is competitive intelligence.
campaigns.objective: Gather competitive intelligence on users of rival sales tools and/or detect data scraping tools.

identities.name: LinkedIn
identities.description: A Microsoft-owned social media platform for professionals, accused of covertly scanning user browsers for installed extensions. The company states the practice is a security measure against data scraping.

identities.name: Fairlinked e.V.
identities.description: An association of commercial LinkedIn users that published the "BrowserGate" report accusing LinkedIn of invasive data collection.

identities.name: Apollo
identities.description: A sales intelligence tool and competitor to LinkedIn's Sales Navigator. LinkedIn was allegedly scanning for the presence of its browser extension.

identities.name: Lusha
identities.description: A sales intelligence tool and competitor to LinkedIn's Sales Navigator. LinkedIn was allegedly scanning for the presence of its browser extension.

identities.name: ZoomInfo
identities.description: A sales intelligence tool and competitor to LinkedIn's Sales Navigator. LinkedIn was allegedly scanning for the presence of its browser extension.

infrastructure.name: LinkedIn Browser Fingerprinting Script
infrastructure.description: A JavaScript file reportedly loaded by LinkedIn's website to check for a list of over 6,200 browser extension IDs to fingerprint users.
